relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Judea hears that the nations received the word.
Peter is challenged by circumcision-boundary concern.
The objection is table fellowship across boundary.
Peter explains in order.
He recounts the heaven-sheet vision in prayer.
He observes the creatures gathered into the vessel.
The voice commands rise and eat.
Peter resists common and unclean.
Heaven answers: do not common what the Divine cleansed.
Threefold repetition seals the message.
Three sent men arrive immediately.
The Spirit commands no distinction, and six siblings witness the crossing.
Cornelius’ messenger story is retold.
Words will bring saving/healing to the whole house.
As Peter begins, Spirit falls as at the beginning.
The Lord’s word about Spirit baptism is remembered.
The equal gift means Peter cannot hinder the Divine.
The assembly quiets and glorifies: the nations receive turning into life.
Stephen’s persecution scatters witness as far as Antioch.
Some speak good news to Greeks also.
The Lord’s hand is with them, and many turn.
Jerusalem sends Barnabas to Antioch.
Barnabas sees grace and encourages purpose of heart.
Full of Spirit and trust, Barnabas gathers more to the Lord.
He seeks Saul in Tarsus.
Antioch becomes a year-long teaching center and naming-place.
Prophets arrive from Jerusalem.
Agabus signals famine through the Spirit.
The disciples respond according to means for Judean siblings.
Barnabas and Saul carry the service to the elders.
